ddls:
CREATE TABLE "boroughs" (
  "borough_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each borough */,
  "borough_name" TEXT /* Name of the borough or city */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the borough or city */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the borough */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the borough */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region of the borough */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of the borough */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("borough_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "covid_cases" (
  "case_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each case */,
  "borough_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the borough where the case was reported */,
  "num_confirmed_cases" INTEGER /* Number of confirmed cases in the borough */,
  "distribution" REAL /* Distribution of cases in the borough as a percentage */,
  "rate_per_100k" REAL /* Rate of infection per 100,000 people in the borough */,
  "num_deaths" INTEGER /* Number of deaths in the borough */,
  "mortality_rate_per_100k" REAL /* Rate of mortality per 100,000 people in the borough */,
  "date_reported" TEXT /* Date when the case was reported */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the case data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_covid_cases_borough_id FOREIGN KEY ("borough_id") REFERENCES boroughs ("borough_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "public_health_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each public health data record */,
  "borough_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the borough where the data was collected */,
  "vaccination_rate" REAL /* Vaccination rate in the borough */,
  "hospitalization_rate" REAL /* Hospitalization rate in the borough */,
  "icu_admission_rate" REAL /* ICU admission rate in the borough */,
  "recovery_rate" REAL /* Recovery rate in the borough */,
  "testing_rate" REAL /* Testing rate in the borough */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_public_health_data_borough_id FOREIGN KEY ("borough_id") REFERENCES boroughs ("borough_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, epidemiologist, public health official) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role that the permission applies to */,
  "permission" TEXT /* Permission granted to the role (e.g., view data, edit data, delete data) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "case_sources" (
  "case_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the case */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("case_id", "source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "public_health_measures" (
  "measure_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each public health measure */,
  "borough_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the borough where the measure was implemented */,
  "measure_name" TEXT /* Name of the public health measure */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the measure */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the measure */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measure_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_public_health_measures_borough_id FOREIGN KEY ("borough_id") REFERENCES boroughs ("borough_id")
)
